response:
Here are comprehensive unit tests for the `is_balanced_parens` function using pytest style:

```python
import pytest


def test_happy_path_simple_balanced():
    """Test simple balanced cases."""
    assert is_balanced_parens("()") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("[]") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("{}") == True


def test_happy_path_nested_balanced():
    """Test nested balanced cases."""
    assert is_balanced_parens("({[]})") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("[{()}]") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("({})[]") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("{[()]}") == True


def test_happy_path_complex_balanced():
    """Test more complex balanced cases."""
    assert is_balanced_parens("()[]{}") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("({[]})()") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("((()))") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("[[[]]]") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("{{{}}}") == True


def test_happy_path_empty_string():
    """Test empty string (should be balanced)."""
    assert is_balanced_parens("") == True


def test_happy_path_no_parentheses():
    """Test string with no parentheses at all."""
    assert is_balanced_parens("abc123") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("hello world") == True


def test_happy_path_mixed_content():
    """Test strings with mixed content (parentheses and other characters)."""
    assert is_balanced_parens("a(b)c") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("test[123]{abc}") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("if (x) { return [1, 2, 3]; }") == True


def test_edge_case_single_opening():
    """Test single opening parentheses."""
    assert is_balanced_parens("(") == False
    assert is_balanced_parens("[") == False
    assert is_balanced_parens("{") == False


def test_edge_case_single_closing():
    """Test single closing parentheses."""
    assert is_balanced_parens(")") == False
    assert is_balanced_parens("]") == False
    assert is_balanced_parens("}") == False


def test_edge_case_mismatched_pairs():
    """Test mismatched parentheses pairs."""
    assert is_balanced_parens("(]") == False
    assert is_balanced_parens("[)") == False
    assert is_balanced_parens("{]") == False
    assert is_balanced_parens("(}") == False


def test_edge_case_wrong_order():
    """Test closing before opening."""
    assert is_balanced_parens(")(") == False
    assert is_balanced_parens("][") == False
    assert is_balanced_parens("}{") == False


def test_edge_case_partial_balance():
    """Test partially balanced strings."""
    assert is_balanced_parens("(()") == False
    assert is_balanced_parens("())") == False
    assert is_balanced_parens("{[}]") == False
    assert is_balanced_parens("([)]") == False


def test_edge_case_deeply_nested_unbalanced():
    """Test deeply nested but unbalanced cases."""
    assert is_balanced_parens("((((())))") == False  # Missing one closing
    assert is_balanced_parens("(((()))))") == False  # Extra closing


def test_edge_case_whitespace():
    """Test strings with whitespace."""
    assert is_balanced_parens(" ( ) ") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("\n\t{}\n") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens(" ( ") == False


def test_edge_case_special_characters():
    """Test strings with special characters."""
    assert is_balanced_parens("(!@#$%^&*())") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("a+b*(c-d)") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("a+b*(c-d") == False


def test_error_case_none_input():
    """Test None input (would raise AttributeError)."""
    with pytest.raises(AttributeError):
        is_balanced_parens(None)
